full_name: Ministry of Children and Family Development
blurb: Supports vulnerable children, youth and families — child protection, foster care and adoption, youth services, and services for children and youth with support needs.

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: Children and youth in B.C. are protected from abuse and neglect
goals.description: The ministry operates the province's child protection system, investigating reports of abuse or neglect and taking the least disruptive action needed to keep a child safe.

main_services.name: Report and respond to a child protection concern

supporting.name: Report suspected child abuse or neglect
supporting.desc: Anyone in B.C. has a legal duty to report if they believe a child or youth under 19 may be abused, neglected, or otherwise in need of protection. Reports go to Provincial Centralized Screening (PCS), available 24 hours a day, 365 days a year; you don't have to give your name.
supporting.users: General public, family members, professionals working with children

legislation.full_name: Child, Family and Community Service Act  -  Duty to report

supporting.name: Get help through the Helpline for Children if you're a child or youth in danger
supporting.desc: A free, confidential 24-hour helpline for children and youth who are unsafe or being abused. Calls are free and you do not have to give your name.

main_services.name: Take part in a child protection legal process

supporting.name: Take part in child protection mediation
supporting.desc: A voluntary, facilitated mediation process that helps families and the ministry reach agreements about a child's safety and care as an alternative to court.
supporting.users: Parents, caregivers and extended family involved in a child protection matter

legislation.full_name: Child, Family and Community Service Act  -  Mediation and dispute resolution

supporting.name: Enter into a Youth Agreement for independent living support
supporting.desc: Youth agreements provide financial and other support to youth aged 16-18 who cannot safely live at home and are not in the ministry's continuing care.

legislation.full_name: Child, Family and Community Service Act  -  Youth agreements

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: Children and youth have stable, connected caregiving and permanency options
goals.description: The ministry recruits and supports caregivers — foster, kinship, respite and contracted agency — and provides adoption services so children who can't live with their birth family have a safe, permanent home.

main_services.name: Become a caregiver for a child or youth in care

supporting.name: Become a foster caregiver
supporting.desc: Welcome a child or youth into your home on a temporary basis while helping them stay connected to family, community and culture, until they can safely return home.

channels.label: Caring for children and youth in B.C.

legislation.full_name: Child, Family and Community Service Act  -  Approval of foster homes

relationships.service: Get a criminal record check through the Criminal Records Review Program

supporting.name: Become a kinship care provider for a child you're related to
supporting.desc: Care for a child or youth you have kinship ties, a relationship with, or a cultural responsibility to, when they need care outside their parents' home.

channels.label: Caring for children and youth in B.C.

supporting.name: Become a contracted agency caregiver
supporting.desc: Work for a community social service agency that provides specialized, complex-needs care for children and youth as an employee.
supporting.users: Individuals seeking employment in child and youth care

channels.label: Caring for children and youth in B.C.

supporting.name: Provide respite care for a child or youth
supporting.desc: Care for a child or youth on a part-time basis to give parents or primary caregivers a break, helping families stay together.

channels.label: Caring for children and youth in B.C.

supporting.name: Get payments and support as a current foster caregiver
supporting.desc: Approved foster caregivers receive a monthly maintenance payment based on home type, must complete mandatory PRIDE In-Service training, and can access insurance and coverage information.

legislation.full_name: Child, Family and Community Service Act  -  Standards for foster homes

supporting.name: Get an affordability payment for a child in your care as a relative
supporting.desc: Relatives caring for a child or youth through the Child in Home of Relative (CIHR) program can receive an annual affordability payment.
supporting.users: Relatives caring for a child outside the formal foster system

supporting.name: Adopt a child from foster care
supporting.desc: Provide a permanent, loving home for a child in government care who is waiting for an adoptive family.

legislation.full_name: Adoption Act  -  Adopting from foster care

supporting.name: Adopt an infant through a licensed adoption agency
supporting.desc: Work with a licensed B.C. adoption agency to adopt an infant; birth parents can help choose the adoptive family and decide how involved they'd like to stay after the adoption.

legislation.full_name: Adoption Act  -  Placement for adoption and consents

supporting.name: Adopt a child from another country
supporting.desc: Work with a licensed B.C. adoption agency to complete the extra planning needed for an intercountry adoption.

legislation.full_name: Adoption Act  -  Interprovincial and intercountry adoptions

supporting.name: Adopt a relative's child or your partner's stepchild
supporting.desc: Apply to court to adopt a child related to you by blood or your partner's child; legal advice is recommended for this kind of adoption.

legislation.full_name: Adoption Act  -  Court proceedings for adoption

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: Children, youth and families get the mental health, disability and family supports they need to thrive
goals.description: The ministry funds mental health services, disability supports, and family and youth programs so children, youth and families can get help before problems become a crisis.

main_services.name: Get mental health support for a child or youth

supporting.name: Access Child and Youth Mental Health (CYMH) services
supporting.desc: Free mental health assessment, treatment and consultation services for children and youth aged 0-18 and their families, delivered through 92 clinics across the province, in person or virtually.

channels.label: Find a CYMH clinic near you

mandate_tags.text: Realign and improve services for children and youth with support and mental health needs (with Ministry of Health)

supporting.name: Get crisis support for a child or youth's mental health
supporting.desc: 24-hour crisis lines for suicide risk, mental health crises, and Indigenous-specific crisis support for children, youth and families.
supporting.users: Children, youth and families in crisis

main_services.name: Get support for a child or youth with a disability or support needs

supporting.name: Get financial support for a child with a disability or support needs
supporting.desc: The new BC Children and Youth Disability Benefit (starting April 2026) and Disability Supplement (starting July 2027) provide direct funding to families of children under 19 with significant, complex or lifelong disabilities, replacing some current programs.
supporting.users: Families of children and youth with disabilities

channels.label: Children and Youth with Support Needs

mandate_tags.text: Realign and improve services for children and youth with support and mental health needs (with Ministry of Health)

relationships.service: Get transition planning support as a youth with a developmental disability

supporting.name: Access community-based therapy and intervention services for your child
supporting.desc: Free and low-cost community-based services — including speech-language, occupational and physio therapy, behavioural and mental health support, and navigation services — for children and youth with support needs, expanding over the next three years.
supporting.users: Children and youth with support needs and their families

channels.label: Children and Youth with Support Needs — services

mandate_tags.text: Realign and improve services for children and youth with support and mental health needs (with Ministry of Health)

supporting.name: Apply for Autism Funding for your child
supporting.desc: Funding for autism intervention therapies for children and youth with an autism spectrum disorder diagnosis. New applications are accepted until March 2027, when the program transitions to the new Disability Benefit.
supporting.users: Families of children and youth with autism spectrum disorder

channels.label: Children and Youth with Support Needs

mandate_tags.text: Realign and improve services for children and youth with support and mental health needs (with Ministry of Health)

main_services.name: Get support to keep families together and help youth transition to adulthood

supporting.name: Get help for family conflict or a youth's behavioural challenges
supporting.desc: Connect with a local Child and Family Service office for programs that help families stay together, solve problems collaboratively, and support youth who are having difficulties.

channels.label: Help for youth and their families
Find a Child and Family Service office

supporting.name: Access Deaf and Hard of Hearing services for your child
supporting.desc: Provincial Deaf and Hard of Hearing Services (PDHSS) support children and youth who are deaf or hard of hearing, and their families.
supporting.users: Deaf and hard of hearing children and youth, and their families

supporting.name: Get support transitioning out of foster care as a young adult
supporting.desc: Young adults leaving government care can get help with education, health, housing, ID, employment and life skills, plus the SAJE program which supports care-experienced young people up to age 27.
supporting.users: Young adults formerly in government care

relationships.service: Get a tuition waiver as a current or former youth in care

supporting.name: Get funding for education as a former youth in care
supporting.desc: The Provincial Tuition Waiver Program and Learning for Future Grant waive tuition and provide grants for current and former youth in care studying at a B.C. public post-secondary institution.
supporting.users: Current and former youth in care

supporting.name: Get income and transition support through the Agreements with Young Adults program
supporting.desc: The Agreements with Young Adults (AYA) program provides income, housing and other support to young adults with care experience during their transition to independence.

goals.title: GOAL 4
goals.short_name: Indigenous children, youth and families exercise jurisdiction over their own child and family services
goals.description: The ministry funds Indigenous-led prevention and family support, and works with First Nations, Métis and Inuit governing bodies as they take up jurisdiction over child and family services in their own communities.

main_services.name: Exercise Indigenous jurisdiction over child and family services

supporting.name: Apply for Indigenous family support funding for your community or agency
supporting.desc: First Nations and Métis-serving agencies can apply for funding to support community-designed prevention and family-support programs that help keep Indigenous families together.

supporting.name: Establish a coordination agreement for Indigenous jurisdiction over child and family services
supporting.desc: Indigenous governing bodies can negotiate coordination agreements with B.C. and Canada to exercise jurisdiction over child and family services for their community under their own Indigenous laws.

legislation.full_name: Child, Family and Community Service Act  -  Indigenous engagement and information sharing (2018/2019 amendments)

mandate_tags.text: Continue implementing legislation honouring Indigenous jurisdiction over child and family services and reduce overrepresentation of Indigenous children in government care

supporting.name: Access Indigenous Child and Youth Mental Health services
supporting.desc: Ministry, delegated and contracted Child and Youth Mental Health teams focused on Indigenous children, youth and families provide culturally grounded assessment, treatment and support.

mandate_tags.text: Realign and improve services for children and youth with support and mental health needs (with Ministry of Health)

supporting.name: Apply for funding under Jordan's Principle for a First Nations child
supporting.desc: All B.C. First Nations children under 19 are guaranteed access to needed health and social services under Jordan's Principle, regardless of jurisdictional funding disputes.

foundational_services.name: Child protection social work and delegated authority administration
foundational_services.desc: Delegates the Director of Child Protection's authority to child protection social workers across 13 service delivery areas and a number of delegated Aboriginal/Indigenous agencies.

legislation.full_name: Child, Family and Community Service Act  -  Delegation of the Director's authority

foundational_services.name: Delegated Indigenous Child and Family Service Agency oversight and agreements management
foundational_services.desc: Manages agreements with Indigenous governing bodies and delegated Indigenous agencies, and maintains the repository of agreements as B.C. transitions toward Indigenous jurisdiction over child and family services.
foundational_services.users: Internal program staff, Indigenous governing bodies

mandate_tags.text: Continue implementing legislation honouring Indigenous jurisdiction over child and family services and reduce overrepresentation of Indigenous children in government care

foundational_services.name: Ministry data, monitoring and quality assurance reporting
foundational_services.desc: Handles complaints, and produces reports, data and quality-assurance information on ministry services for clients and service providers.

mandate_tags.text: Review all MCFD programs and initiatives with the Ministry of Finance to ensure they remain relevant, efficient, and improve outcomes for vulnerable British Columbians
Lead work to design and implement a comprehensive child well-being plan and outcomes framework across ministries and agencies

mandate_legend.text: Review all Ministry of Children and Family Development programs and initiatives with the Ministry of Finance to ensure they remain relevant, efficient, and improve the lives of vulnerable British Columbians
mandate_legend.mapping: -> Ministry data, monitoring and quality assurance reporting

mandate_legend.text: Continue implementing B.C.'s historic legislation honouring Indigenous jurisdiction over the care of children and families, and continue to address and reduce the overrepresentation of Indigenous children in government care
mandate_legend.mapping: -> Establish a coordination agreement for Indigenous jurisdiction over child and family services; Delegated Indigenous Child and Family Service Agency oversight and agreements management

mandate_legend.text: Lead work to design and implement a comprehensive child well-being plan and outcomes framework that aligns services across ministries and agencies, prioritizing a service model that brings the most vulnerable children and families to the front of the line
mandate_legend.mapping: -> Ministry data, monitoring and quality assurance reporting

mandate_legend.text: Lead work with the Ministry of Health, Indigenous peoples, stakeholders and people with lived experience to realign and improve services for children and youth with support and mental health needs
mandate_legend.mapping: -> Access Child and Youth Mental Health (CYMH) services; Get financial support for a child with a disability or support needs; Access community-based therapy and intervention services for your child; Apply for Autism Funding for your child; Access Indigenous Child and Youth Mental Health services
